A.missing: asserts dead person literally says words to medium as fact, not belief; no 'people think' framing
A.reason: Renders relay between dead and living but states it as literal fact, missing the belief/purported nature and the intermediary being the defining living person.

B.reason: Medium can hear the dead when others cannot, and relays their words to the living — captures intermediary role between living and dead.

C.missing: does not mark that message is FROM the dead person in the relay clause
C.reason: People think this one can hear the dead's words and he says words to the living — intermediary between living and dead, framed as belief.

D.missing: no death/dead, no living/dead intermediary; just someone thinks of someone and speaks to another
D.reason: Generic communication skeleton with no reference to the dead; fits countless concepts.

E.missing: belief framing softer; roles a bit loose
E.reason: A dead person can say something to this one who relays to the living, framed as what some people think — captures the intermediary role.
